heading: Within State allocations
label: 3163 Within State allocations

A, title III, § 3515134 Stat. 407Mar. 27, 2020“(a).—Administrative CostsNotwithstanding section 128(b)(4) of the Workforce Innovation [and] Opportunity Act (), of the total amount allocated to a local area (including the total amount allotted to a single State local area) under subtitle B of title I of such Act ( et seq.) for program year 2019, not more than 20 percent of the total amount may be used for the administrative costs of carrying out local workforce investment activities under chapter 2 [ et seq.] or chapter 3 [ et seq.] of subtitle B of title I of such Act, if the portion of the total amount that exceeds 10 percent of the total amount is used to respond to a qualifying emergency.29 U.S.C. 3163(b)(4)29 U.S.C. 315129 U.S.C. 316129 U.S.C. 3171“(b)Rapid Response Activities.—“(1).—Statewide rapid responseOf the funds reserved by a Governor for program year 2019 for statewide activities under section 128(a) of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act () that remain unobligated, such funds may be used for statewide rapid response activities as described in section 134(a)(2)(A) of such Act () for responding to a qualifying emergency.29 U.S.C. 3163(a)29 U.S.C. 3174(a)(2)(A)“(2).—Local boardsOf the funds reserved by a Governor for program year 2019 under section 133(a)(2) of such Act () that remain unobligated, such funds may be released within 30 days after the date of enactment of this Act [] to the local boards most impacted by the coronavirus at the determination of the Governor for rapid response activities related to responding to a qualifying emergency.29 U.S.C. 3173(a)(2)Mar. 27, 2020“(c).—DefinitionsExcept as otherwise provided, the terms in this section have the meanings given the terms in section 3 of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act ().”29 U.S.C. 3102[For definitions of “qualifying emergency” and “coronavirus” as used in , set out above, see , set out in a note under , Education.]section 3515 of Pub. children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Governor shall reserve not more than 15 percent of each of the amounts allotted to the State under and paragraphs (1)(B) and (2)(B) of for a fiscal year for statewide workforce investment activities.section 3162(b)(1)(C) of this titlesection 3172(b) of this title

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: 2 Use of funds
children.text: Regardless of whether the reserved amounts were allotted under , or under paragraph (1)(B) or (2)(B) of , the Governor may use the reserved amounts to carry out statewide activities under or statewide employment and training activities, for adults or dislocated workers, under .section 3162(b)(1)(C) of this titlesection 3172(b) of this titlesection 3164(b) of this titlesection 3174(a) of this title

children.heading: Methods
children.label: 1 Methods
children.text: The Governor, acting in accordance with the State plan, and after consulting with chief elected officials and local boards in the local areas, shall allocate the funds that are allotted to the State for youth activities and statewide workforce investment activities under and are not reserved under subsection (a), in accordance with paragraph (2) or (3).section 3162(b)(1)(C) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: 33⅓ percent of the funds on the basis described in ;section 3162(b)(1)(C)(ii)(I) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: 33⅓ percent of the funds on the basis described in ; andsection 3162(b)(1)(C)(ii)(II) of this title

children.label: III
children.text: 33⅓ percent of the funds on the basis described in clauses (ii)(III) and (iii) of .section 3162(b)(1)(C) of this title

children.heading: Minimum percentage
children.label: ii Minimum percentage
children.text: The local area shall not receive an allocation percentage for a fiscal year that is less than 90 percent of the average allocation percentage of the local area for the 2 preceding fiscal years. Amounts necessary for increasing such allocations to local areas to comply with the preceding sentence shall be obtained by ratably reducing the allocations to be made to other local areas under this subparagraph.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: iii Definition
children.text: In this subparagraph, the term “allocation percentage”, used with respect to fiscal year 2015 or a subsequent fiscal year, means a percentage of the funds referred to in clause (i), received through an allocation made under this subparagraph, for the fiscal year. The term, used with respect to fiscal year 2013 or 2014, means a percentage of the funds referred to in section 128(b)(1) of the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 [] (as in effect on the day before ), received through an allocation made under paragraph (2) or (3) of section 128(b) of the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 (as so in effect), for the fiscal year 2013 or 2014, respectively.29 U.S.C. 2853(b)(1)July 22, 2014

children.label: i
children.text: references in to a State shall be deemed to be references to a local area;section 3162(b) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: references in to all States shall be deemed to be references to all local areas in the State involved; andsection 3162(b) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: except as described in clause (i), references in to the term “excess number” shall be considered to be references to the term as defined in .section 3162(b)(1) of this titlesection 3162(b)(2) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a portion equal to not less than 70 percent of the funds in accordance with paragraph (2)(A); and

children.label: I
children.text: excess youth poverty in urban, rural, and suburban local areas; and

children.label: II
children.text: excess unemployment above the State average in urban, rural, and suburban local areas; and

children.label: ii
children.text: was developed by the State board and approved by the Secretary as part of the State plan.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Of the amount allocated to a local area under this subsection and for a fiscal year, not more than 10 percent of the amount may be used by the local board involved for the administrative costs of carrying out local workforce investment activities under this subpart or subpart 3.section 3173(b) of this title

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: B Use of funds
children.text: Funds made available for administrative costs under subparagraph (A) may be used for the administrative costs of any of the local workforce investment activities described in this subpart or subpart 3, regardless of whether the funds were allocated under this subsection or .section 3173(b) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Governor may, in accordance with this subsection and after consultation with the State board, reallocate to eligible local areas within the State amounts that are made available to local areas from allocations made under this section or a corresponding provision of the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 for youth workforce investment activities (referred to individually in this subsection as a “local allocation”) and that are available for reallocation.

children.heading: Amount
children.label: 2 Amount
children.text: The amount available for reallocation for a program year is equal to the amount by which the unobligated balance of the local allocation, at the end of the program year prior to the program year for which the determination under this paragraph is made, exceeds 20 percent of such allocation for the prior program year.

children.heading: Reallocation
children.label: 3 Reallocation
children.text: In making reallocations to eligible local areas of amounts available pursuant to paragraph (2) for a program year, the Governor shall allocate to each eligible local area within the State an amount based on the relative amount of the local allocation for the program year for which the determination is made, as compared to the total amount of the local allocations for all eligible local areas in the State for such program year.

children.heading: Eligibility
children.label: 4 Eligibility
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, an eligible local area means a local area that does not have an amount available for reallocation under paragraph (2) for the program year for which the determination under paragraph (2) is made.
